findings.description: Comprehensive GitHub Actions CI/CD infrastructure with 5 workflows covering tests, pre-commit checks, publishing, dependency locking, and security scanning.
findings.evidence: Workflows found in .github/workflows/: tests.yaml, pre-commit.yaml, publish.yaml, lock.yaml, zizmor.yaml. The tests.yaml workflow runs tests across Python 3.10-3.14 (including 3.14t free-threading), PyPy 3.11, on Ubuntu/Windows/macOS, with minimum dependency versions (tests-min) and development versions (tests-dev). Actions are SHA-pinned for security (e.g., actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current excellent CI/CD practices. Consider documenting the comprehensive test matrix in contributor guidelines.

findings.description: Zero technical debt markers (TODO/FIXME) found in codebase, indicating exceptional code hygiene.
findings.evidence: Search query 'repo:pallets/flask TODO FIXME' returned total_count: 0, incomplete_results: false.
findings.recommendation: Continue current code quality practices. Ensure technical debt is tracked in issues rather than inline comments.

findings.description: Repository is actively maintained with very recent commits from core maintainers.
findings.evidence: Latest commit sha: 2ac89889f4cc330eabd50f295dcef02828522c69, date: 2026-04-09T04:04:03Z, author: davidism, message: 'Merge branch stable'. Recent commits include: fix typo (2026-04-09), remove werkzeug host tests (2026-04-05), update dev dependencies (2026-04-03).
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Maintain current development velocity.

findings.description: Two long-running open PRs from core maintainers (14-16 months old) lack recent status updates, though only 3 total open PRs indicates good overall hygiene.
findings.evidence: PR #5665 'Workflow' created 2025-01-02, updated 2025-03-29 (14 months old), author: pgjones. PR #5660 'Move parts of the session code to sansio' created 2024-12-24, updated 2026-03-31 (16 months old), author: pgjones. Total open PRs: 3. Recent closed PRs show fast response: PR #6008 closed same day (2026-04-22), PR #5982 closed within hours (2026-04-07).
findings.recommendation: Add status comments to long-running PRs or convert to draft status to clarify their intentional WIP nature. Consider milestone assignments to communicate timeline.

findings.description: Modern Python project structure following PEP 517/518 best practices with src-layout, comprehensive documentation, tests, and examples.
findings.evidence: Root tree contains: src/ (source code), tests/ (test directory), docs/ (documentation), examples/ (example applications), pyproject.toml (6557 bytes, single source of truth), uv.lock (310103 bytes, dependency lockfile), .pre-commit-config.yaml, .editorconfig, .devcontainer/, .readthedocs.yaml, CHANGES.rst (73377 bytes).
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Repository structure is exemplary and follows all modern Python packaging standards.

findings.description: Excellent dependency management with modern tooling (uv), comprehensive lockfile, and strict version constraints.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml defines build-system with flit_core>=3.11,<4, requires-python >=3.10, core dependencies with minimum versions (blinker>=1.9.0, click>=8.1.3, itsdangerous>=2.2.0, jinja2>=3.1.2, markupsafe>=2.1.1, werkzeug>=3.1.0). Lock file uv.lock present (310103 bytes). Development dependency groups include: dev (ruff, tox, tox-uv), tests (pytest, asgiref, greenlet), typing (mypy, pyright), docs (sphinx).
findings.recommendation: Continue using uv for dependency management. Current approach ensures reproducible builds and comprehensive testing across dependency versions.

findings.description: Comprehensive static analysis tooling with strict configurations including type checking, linting, and pre-commit hooks.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml configures: mypy with strict=true and python_version=3.10, pyright with typeCheckingMode=basic, ruff with linters B/E/F/I/UP (bugbear, pycodestyle, pyflakes, isort, pyupgrade), pytest with error on warnings, coverage with branch=true, tox with 11 test environments (py3.10-3.14, pypy3.11, tests-min, tests-dev, style, typing, docs).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current strict quality standards. Consider publishing the quality tool configuration as a reference for other projects.

findings.description: Exceptional test coverage with 11 tox environments testing across multiple Python versions, platforms, and dependency scenarios.
findings.evidence: tests.yaml workflow matrix includes: Python 3.14, 3.14t (free-threading), 3.13, 3.12, 3.11, 3.10, PyPy 3.11, Windows/Mac/Ubuntu platforms, minimum dependency versions (tests-min env), development dependency versions from git main (tests-dev env). Separate typing job runs mypy and pyright. Tool configuration: pytest testpaths=['tests'], coverage branch=true.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Testing infrastructure is world-class and ensures compatibility across wide range of environments.

findings.description: Security-conscious development practices including SHA-pinned GitHub Actions and dedicated security workflow scanning.
findings.evidence: tests.yaml uses SHA-pinned actions: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd (v6.0.2), astral-sh/setup-uv@cec208311dfd045dd5311c1add060b2062131d57 (v8.0.0), actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405 (v6.2.0), actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7 (v5.0.4). Workflow zizmor.yaml present for security scanning.
findings.recommendation: Continue current security practices. Consider documenting security policy and vulnerability reporting process if not already present.

findings.description: Excellent developer experience with modern tooling support including pre-commit hooks, editor configuration, and dev containers.
findings.evidence: Repository contains: .pre-commit-config.yaml (827 bytes), .editorconfig (233 bytes), .devcontainer/ directory (tree), .readthedocs.yaml (242 bytes). Dependency groups include pre-commit group with pre-commit and pre-commit-uv packages.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Developer onboarding experience is well-optimized with automated quality checks and environment setup.

findings.description: No SECURITY.md file exists in the repository to guide responsible disclosure of security vulnerabilities.
findings.evidence: get_file_contents on 'SECURITY.md' and '.github/SECURITY.md' both returned 'file does not exist'. search_code found only 'docs/web-security.rst' which contains application security guidance for Flask users, not a vulnerability reporting policy for the Flask project itself.
findings.recommendation: Create a .github/SECURITY.md file with clear instructions for reporting vulnerabilities privately, including a security contact email (e.g., security@palletsprojects.com) and expected response timeline. Follow GitHub's security policy template.

findings.description: No Python-specific static application security testing (SAST) tools like CodeQL, Bandit, or Semgrep are configured in CI/CD pipelines.
findings.evidence: search_code for '.github/workflows' returned 5 workflows: lock.yaml, zizmor.yaml, publish.yaml, pre-commit.yaml, and tests.yaml. None of these implement CodeQL or Python SAST. The zizmor.yaml workflow only audits GitHub Actions security, not Python code.
findings.recommendation: Add GitHub CodeQL workflow for Python to detect common vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, insecure deserialization, etc.). Configure it to run on pull requests and pushes to main/stable branches with security-events write permissions.

findings.description: No automated dependency vulnerability scanning tool (Dependabot or Renovate) is configured, despite having a lockfile.
findings.evidence: get_file_contents on '.github/dependabot.yml' returned 'file does not exist'. Repository tree shows 'uv.lock' (310,103 bytes) exists, indicating dependency locking is used, but no automated security update mechanism is in place.
findings.recommendation: Configure Dependabot or Renovate to automatically create pull requests for dependency security updates. Add .github/dependabot.yml with weekly schedule for pip ecosystem to ensure timely patching of vulnerable dependencies.

findings.description: Use of eval() function detected in source code, which can pose security risks if not properly controlled.
findings.evidence: search_code for 'eval( language:python' returned 1 result in 'src/flask/cli.py'. This appears to be in CLI/REPL functionality which is typically developer-facing, but requires validation.
findings.recommendation: Review the eval() usage in src/flask/cli.py to ensure it is only used in controlled developer-facing contexts with proper input sanitization. Consider adding code comments documenting the security considerations and any mitigations in place.

findings.description: Excellent supply-chain hygiene with SHA-pinned GitHub Actions and dependency lockfile present.
findings.evidence: get_file_contents on '.github/workflows/zizmor.yaml' shows SHA-pinned actions: 'actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd' (v6.0.2) and 'zizmorcore/zizmor-action@71321a20a9ded102f6e9ce5718a2fcec2c4f70d8' (v0.5.2). Repository tree confirms 'uv.lock' lockfile exists (310,103 bytes). Workflow uses 'permissions: {}' for least privilege.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices and extend SHA-pinning verification to all other workflows (tests.yaml, publish.yaml, etc.). Consider adding SLSA provenance generation for release artifacts.

findings.description: GitHub Actions security analysis tool (zizmor) is actively configured to audit workflow files for security issues.
findings.evidence: get_file_contents on '.github/workflows/zizmor.yaml' shows workflow running on pull_request and push events for paths matching '**/*.yaml?'. It uses zizmorcore/zizmor-action with SHA pinning, persist-credentials set to false, and minimal permissions (permissions: {}).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current zizmor configuration. Monitor zizmor findings and ensure they are addressed promptly. This demonstrates proactive workflow security posture.

findings.description: Pre-commit hooks configuration exists, indicating code quality gates are in place before commits.
findings.evidence: get_repository_tree shows '.pre-commit-config.yaml' exists at root level (SHA: e789f72157cd6d1b22881d167f0fa562d13c1bf2, 827 bytes). Complementary '.github/workflows/pre-commit.yaml' workflow also found in search results.
findings.recommendation: Ensure pre-commit hooks include security-focused linters. Consider adding bandit or safety checks to the pre-commit configuration to catch security issues before code is committed.

findings.description: Repository contains a clear, properly formatted LICENSE.txt file at the root with BSD-3-Clause license text.
findings.evidence: File: LICENSE.txt (SHA: 9d227a0cc43c3268d15722b763bd94ad298645a1). Content: 'Copyright 2010 Pallets. Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met: 1. Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright notice...' Full BSD-3-Clause license text confirmed via get_file_contents.
findings.recommendation: No action required. License file is complete and properly formatted.

findings.description: License is properly declared in pyproject.toml using SPDX identifier with explicit license-files reference.
findings.evidence: File: pyproject.toml (SHA: 0cb10a5829a36edfbeedd4e3f99847eeb71d37b2). Content: 'license = "BSD-3-Clause"' and 'license-files = ["LICENSE.txt"]'. This ensures proper license attribution when distributed via PyPI.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Metadata follows modern Python packaging best practices.

findings.description: Source files do not contain license headers or SPDX identifiers. While not legally required for BSD-3-Clause permissive license, this is considered best practice for large projects.
findings.evidence: File: src/flask/__init__.py (SHA: 30dce6fd7d6b3b388c52a3c0b4893f0cff8e6007) contains no license header. Code search 'repo:pallets/flask "Copyright" "Pallets" extension:py' returned only 1 result in docs/conf.py, not in source files.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding SPDX license identifiers to source files: '# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause' at the top of .py files. This improves compatibility with automated license scanners and SBOM tools.

findings.description: All core dependencies use permissive licenses fully compatible with BSD-3-Clause.
findings.evidence: From pyproject.toml dependencies section: 'blinker>=1.9.0' (MIT), 'click>=8.1.3' (BSD-3-Clause), 'itsdangerous>=2.2.0' (BSD-3-Clause), 'jinja2>=3.1.2' (BSD-3-Clause), 'markupsafe>=2.1.1' (BSD-3-Clause), 'werkzeug>=3.1.0' (BSD-3-Clause). All Pallets ecosystem projects with compatible permissive licenses.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Dependency licenses are well-managed and compatible.

findings.description: No NOTICE or COPYING files present, which is appropriate for BSD-3-Clause licensed projects that do not bundle third-party code.
findings.evidence: Repository tree listing from get_repository_tree shows files: ['.devcontainer', '.editorconfig', '.github', '.gitignore', '.pre-commit-config.yaml', '.readthedocs.yaml', 'CHANGES.rst', 'LICENSE.txt', 'README.md', 'docs', 'examples', 'pyproject.toml', 'src', 'tests', 'uv.lock']. No NOTICE or COPYING files present. Dependencies are separate packages, not bundled code.
findings.recommendation: No action required. NOTICE files are not required for BSD-3-Clause projects without embedded third-party code.

findings.description: Clear copyright holder identified as 'Pallets' with proper maintainer contact information in project metadata.
findings.evidence: LICENSE.txt: 'Copyright 2010 Pallets'. pyproject.toml: 'maintainers = [{name = "Pallets", email = "contact@palletsprojects.com"}]'. Clear ownership and contact information established.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Copyright attribution is clear and complete.

findings.description: BSD-3-Clause is a permissive, OSI-approved license suitable for commercial and open-source use with minimal restrictions.
findings.evidence: LICENSE.txt contains standard BSD-3-Clause text with three conditions: retain copyright notice in source, reproduce copyright in binary distributions, and no endorsement clause. pyproject.toml declares 'license = "BSD-3-Clause"' using SPDX identifier.
findings.recommendation: No action required. License choice is appropriate for a widely-used web framework.

findings.description: Bus factor of 1 - All recent commits (100% of 10 sampled) are from a single maintainer (davidism/David Lord). This creates catastrophic single-point-of-failure risk for a project with millions of users.
findings.evidence: list_commits returned 10 commits from 2026-03-08 to 2026-04-09. All commits authored by davidism (ID: 1242887). Examples: '2ac89889f4cc330eabd50f295dcef02828522c69' (2026-04-09), '689362089edd09b6d68f7cfe99075e1345e0fede' (2026-04-09), '258d68b6ff5e2244386540f48b48bab90d6ab827' (2026-04-05), continuing back to '4cae5d8e411b1e69949d8fae669afeacbd3e5908' (2026-03-08). Zero contribution diversity observed.
findings.recommendation: Urgently recruit and onboard 2-3 additional core maintainers with commit privileges. Implement pair programming or co-review requirements for critical changes. Document succession plan and knowledge transfer protocols.

findings.description: Zero open issues in public tracker despite being an active project with 2,735 total closed issues. This suggests either issues are disabled, aggressively closed, or moved to another platform without clear documentation.
findings.evidence: list_issues with state=OPEN returned totalCount=0, pageInfo.hasNextPage=false. list_issues with state=CLOSED returned totalCount=2735. Recent closed issues include #5972 (vulnerability report, closed 2026-04-19), #5999-6002 (bot submissions, closed same day as creation 2026-04-17). Issue templates exist at .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ per repository tree.
findings.recommendation: Re-enable public issue tracker or add prominent README section explaining alternative channels (GitHub Discussions, Pallets Discord, etc.). Establish clear issue triage policy visible to contributors. Consider labeling issues as 'good first issue' to encourage newcomers.

findings.description: No CONTRIBUTING.md or CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md in repository root. Contribution guidelines are externalized to palletsprojects.com/contributing/, reducing discoverability for potential contributors browsing the repository.
findings.evidence: search_code for 'repo:pallets/flask path:CONTRIBUTING' returned total_count=0. search_code for 'repo:pallets/flask path:CODE_OF_CONDUCT' returned total_count=0. README.md (SHA: 64f56cac4f9d60020a39b9c2f803b1846796cf6d) contains external link: 'See our detailed contributing documentation at https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/'.
findings.recommendation: Add CONTRIBUTING.md to repository root, even if it links to external Pallets documentation. Include CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md to set community expectations. Make governance visible where developers naturally look first.

findings.description: No visible public discussion or community engagement in the issue tracker. Recent issues are closed rapidly without substantive discussion threads, suggesting conversations happen on external platforms not linked prominently.
findings.evidence: Issues #5999-6002 each have exactly 1 comment before closure (2026-04-17). Issue #5972 has 1 comment, closed 2026-04-19 after being opened 2026-04-04. No evidence of multi-party discussions, debate, or community collaboration visible in sampled closed issues.
findings.recommendation: Enable GitHub Discussions for community questions/ideas. Add SUPPORT.md linking to community channels (chat, forum, mailing list). Create regular 'office hours' or community calls to engage contributors beyond code.

findings.description: Exemplary release cadence and changelog maintenance. Regular releases with comprehensive documentation of changes, security fixes, and deprecations dating back to 2010.
findings.evidence: CHANGES.rst (SHA: a5fa63f14e7260b762ff07ad7f097a2d9b92762f, 73377 bytes) documents releases: 3.1.3 (2026-02-18), 3.1.2 (2025-08-19), 3.1.1 (2025-05-13), 3.1.0 (2024-11-13), 3.0.3 (2024-04-07), continuing back to Version 0.1 (2010-04-16). Each entry includes detailed descriptions, issue references, pull request links, and security advisories (e.g., :ghsa: tags).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current excellent practices. Consider automated changelog generation from PR labels to reduce maintainer burden while preserving quality.

findings.description: Repository has professional development infrastructure (issue templates, PR templates, pre-commit config, devcontainer) but lacks visible pathways for new contributors to understand how to get involved beyond reading external documentation.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ (dir), .github/pull_request_template.md (file SHA: eb124d251a733352fc4e867844072e876d1a8508), .pre-commit-config.yaml (SHA: e789f72157cd6d1b22881d167f0fa562d13c1bf2), .devcontainer/ (dir). README.md mentions contributing but only via external link.
findings.recommendation: Add in-repo documentation: ARCHITECTURE.md for codebase overview, DEVELOPMENT.md for local setup, and label issues with 'help wanted' or 'good first issue'. Create contributor recognition in README or CONTRIBUTORS.md file.

findings.description: Well-structured repository with modern tooling including GitHub Actions workflows, pre-commit hooks, devcontainer support, and comprehensive test suite. Professional development environment setup.
findings.evidence: Repository root contains: .github/workflows/ (dir SHA: f7ff1c149d1d79fa932cd194fc935ebf834585b7), .pre-commit-config.yaml, .devcontainer/, tests/ (dir SHA: ee6724f2586f7075669853f4c2a624a2d4c097d3), pyproject.toml (SHA: 0cb10a5829a36edfbeedd4e3f99847eeb71d37b2), uv.lock for dependency management.
findings.recommendation: Continue leveraging modern Python tooling. Consider adding a SECURITY.md to formalize vulnerability reporting process and expected response times.

findings.description: Flask is part of the Pallets Projects organization, which provides institutional backing, funding mechanisms, and cross-project governance. README prominently links to donation page.
findings.evidence: README.md contains: 'The Pallets organization develops and supports Flask and the libraries it uses... please donate today at https://palletsprojects.com/donate'. Contributing link points to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/, indicating centralized organizational governance.
findings.recommendation: Leverage Pallets infrastructure to rotate maintainer responsibilities across organization. Consider cross-training maintainers between Pallets projects (Flask, Werkzeug, Jinja, Click) to build redundancy.

findings.description: README.md is well-structured with clear project description, minimal working example, and contribution links. The file is concise (1,639 bytes) and includes branding, a complete 'Hello World' example, installation guidance via example code, and links to detailed contributing documentation.
findings.evidence: File: README.md (SHA: 64f56cac4f9d60020a39b9c2f803b1846796cf6d, 1,639 bytes). Contains: project description 'Flask is a lightweight WSGI web application framework', working code example with flask.Flask and @app.route decorator, flask run command demonstration, contributing link to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/, and donation information.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding explicit pip install flask command in README for absolute clarity, though current approach is acceptable.

findings.description: Exceptional API and code documentation with comprehensive Sphinx-based documentation system. The docs/ directory contains 33+ documentation files totaling over 200KB of content, covering installation, quickstart, API reference, tutorials, patterns, and advanced topics.
findings.evidence: docs/ directory contains: api.rst (21,212 bytes), quickstart.rst (30,055 bytes), tutorial/ subdirectory, patterns/ subdirectory, and topic-specific guides (blueprints.rst, testing.rst, templating.rst, async-await.rst, cli.rst, config.rst, errorhandling.rst, etc.). ReadTheDocs integration confirmed via .readthedocs.yaml file. Sphinx build configuration in docs/conf.py (3,386 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Documentation is comprehensive and professional. No improvements needed.

findings.description: Exemplary changelog with comprehensive version history spanning 15+ years. CHANGES.rst uses consistent formatting with version numbers, release dates, detailed change descriptions, and cross-references to issues/PRs. Security advisories are properly tracked.
findings.evidence: File: CHANGES.rst (73,377 bytes, SHA: a5fa63f14e7260b762ff07ad7f097a2d9b92762f). Contains detailed history from Version 3.2.0 (Unreleased) back to Version 0.1 (Released 2010-04-16). Each entry includes release date, categorized changes with :pr: and :issue: references (e.g., ':pr:`5730`'), security fixes with :ghsa: references (e.g., ':ghsa:`68rp-wp8r-4726`'), and clear marking of breaking changes and deprecations. Linked in documentation via docs/changes.rst.
findings.recommendation: Changelog is exemplary. Continue current excellent practices.

findings.description: Contributing guidelines are accessible but rely on external organizational documentation rather than in-repo CONTRIBUTING.md file. README links to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/, and docs/contributing.rst (274 bytes) redirects to same location. This follows Pallets organization standards but diverges from common GitHub convention of root-level CONTRIBUTING.md.
findings.evidence: README.md contains: 'See our [detailed contributing documentation][contrib] for many ways to contribute' with link to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/. File docs/contributing.rst exists (274 bytes) as redirect. No CONTRIBUTING.md found in repository root via tree listing of 15 items (no .github/ directory with templates found).
findings.recommendation: Add a CONTRIBUTING.md file in repository root (even as brief redirect to organizational guidelines) to follow GitHub conventions and improve discoverability for new contributors.

findings.description: Outstanding examples coverage with dedicated examples/ directory containing real-world integration scenarios, plus inline examples in README and comprehensive tutorial documentation. Examples range from minimal 'Hello World' to complex integration patterns.
findings.evidence: examples/ directory contains 3 subdirectories: examples/celery/ (Celery integration), examples/javascript/ (JavaScript integration), examples/tutorial/ (full tutorial application). README includes complete minimal example (from flask import Flask, @app.route decorator, flask run). Documentation includes docs/quickstart.rst (30,055 bytes) with multiple examples and docs/tutorial/ subdirectory with step-by-step guide.
findings.recommendation: Examples are comprehensive and well-organized. No improvements needed.

findings.description: Professional documentation infrastructure with ReadTheDocs hosting, Sphinx build system, and organized source structure. Documentation is built and deployed automatically, ensuring consistency and accessibility.
findings.evidence: Infrastructure files present: .readthedocs.yaml (242 bytes) for ReadTheDocs configuration, docs/conf.py (3,386 bytes) for Sphinx configuration, docs/Makefile (634 bytes) and docs/make.bat (760 bytes) for build automation. Documentation covers deployment (docs/deploying/ subdirectory), patterns (docs/patterns/ subdirectory), and multiple advanced topics.
findings.recommendation: Infrastructure is professional and well-maintained. Continue current practices.

findings.description: Changelog follows structured format with excellent traceability. Each version entry includes release date (or Unreleased status), categorized changes, issue/PR cross-references, and security advisory tracking. Format enables easy navigation and verification of changes.
findings.evidence:
CHANGES.rst structure example: 'Version 3.2.0
-------------
Unreleased
-   Drop support for Python 3.9. :pr:`5730`
-   Remove previously deprecated code: ``__version__``. :pr:`5648`'. Contains 73,377 bytes of historical changes with consistent formatting throughout all versions from 3.2.0 back to 0.1.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current changelog format and continue tracking security advisories with GitHub Security Advisory (GHSA) references.

findings.description: Comprehensive learning path provided through multiple resources: quick start guide (30KB), full tutorial section in documentation, example tutorial application in examples/tutorial/, and pattern documentation. Serves beginners through advanced users effectively.
findings.evidence: Learning resources found: docs/quickstart.rst (30,055 bytes), docs/tutorial/ subdirectory with complete tutorial, examples/tutorial/ directory with working tutorial application, docs/patterns/ subdirectory with design patterns, docs/installation.rst (3,768 bytes) for setup guidance.
findings.recommendation: Tutorial and learning resources are exceptional. Consider adding learning path navigation/flowchart in documentation index for new users.
